name: Route of the Molino de La Negra – circular from Valencia de Alcántara (PR-CC 97)
name_original: Ruta del Molino de La Negra – circular desde Valencia de Alcántara (PR-CC 97)
region_intro: Hiking around Valencia De Alcántara offers diverse landscapes characterized by impressive granitic formations known as "Canchales" and extensive "Dehesa" woodlands of holm and cork oaks. The region's topography includes the Sierra Fría and Sierra de San Pedro mountain ranges, alongside deep river channels like the Sever river, which forms a natural border with Portugal. This area is also notable for its significant megalithic complex, featuring numerous well-preserved dolmens and menhirs. Best hiking trails around Valencia De Alcántara The most popular hiking route is Galegos Hiking Trail (PR 2) — Serra de São Mamede, a moderate 7.3 miles (11.7 km) trail that takes about 3 hours 14 minutes to complete. This route explores the natural beauty near the Serra de São Mamede Natural Park. Another top favourite among local hikers is Route of the Dolmens of Aceña de la Borrega, a moderate 4.7 miles (7.6 km) path. This trail leads through ancient megalithic sites and distinctive granite formations. Local hikers also love the Route of the Molino de La Negra – circular from Valencia de Alcántara (PR-CC 97), a 9.3 miles (15.0 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 4 hours 2 minutes. Hiking around Valencia De Alcántara is defined by granitic formations, "Dehesa" woodlands, and river systems. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult trails. The routes in Valencia De Alcántara are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 270 reviews. More than 950 hikers have used komoot to explore Valencia De Alcántara's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Valencia de Alcántara?
A: Valencia de Alcántara offers a wide variety of hiking experiences, with over 90 routes recorded on komoot. These include 34 easy, 54 moderate, and 4 difficult trails, catering to different fitness levels and preferences.

Q: What kind of natural landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Valencia de Alcántara?
A: The region is characterized by impressive granitic formations known as "Canchales," extensive "Dehesa" woodlands of holm and cork oaks, and deep river channels like the Sever river. You'll also encounter varied topography from the Sierra Fría and Sierra de San Pedro mountain ranges, creating a diverse and picturesque environment.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Valencia de Alcántara?
A: Yes, Valencia de Alcántara has 34 easy routes suitable for families. For a gentle introduction to the area's history and nature, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31429058" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Fontanera – Olive Oil Mill Museum loop from Santa Maria de Marvão&lt;/a&gt;, which is an easy 6.2 km trail.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Valencia de Alcántara?
A: Absolutely. Many trails in the region are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. An excellent example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990810766/route-of-the-molino-de-la-negra-circular-from-valencia-de-alcantara-pr-cc-97" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Molino de La Negra – circular from Valencia de Alcántara (PR-CC 97)&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 15 km loop that takes you through varied terrain.

Q: What historical sites or landmarks can I explore on a hike?
A: Valencia de Alcántara is renowned for its significant megalithic complex. You can explore ancient dolmens and menhirs on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990371344/route-of-the-dolmens-of-acena-de-la-borrega" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Dolmens of Aceña de la Borrega&lt;/a&gt;. Other notable historical sites include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/795936" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marvão Castle&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/659213" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic Village of Marvão&lt;/a&gt;, both accessible from nearby trails.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Valencia de Alcántara?
A: Spring is highly recommended for hiking in Valencia de Alcántara. During this season, the landscape transforms with vibrant green grass and a profusion of red, white, and yellow wildflowers under clear blue skies, offering a particularly beautiful experience.

Q: Are there any trails that offer views of the border with Portugal?
A: Yes, the region is located near the Spanish-Portuguese border, and some trails offer views of the neighboring country. The Sever river itself forms a natural border. For a cross-border experience, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38223944" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rio Sever loop from Santa Maria de Marvão&lt;/a&gt;, which explores the border area.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Valencia de Alcántara?
A: The trails in Valencia de Alcántara are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 270 reviews. Hikers often praise the unique granite formations, the historical megalithic sites, and the tranquil beauty of the Dehesa woodlands.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: While most routes are easy to moderate, there are 4 difficult trails for those seeking a greater challenge. These routes often feature steeper ascents and more rugged terrain, providing a rewarding experience for experienced hikers.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter on the trails?
A: While hiking, you might spot local farm animals such as horses, cows, and sheep. The region is also a vital habitat for various bird species, including impressive birds of prey like the Spanish imperial eagle, black stork, and griffon vulture.

Q: Are there any natural parks nearby that I can explore?
A: Yes, Valencia de Alcántara borders the Serra de São Mamede Natural Park in Portugal and the Sierra de San Pedro Natural Park in Spain. These protected areas are rich in biodiversity and offer additional opportunities for exploring diverse flora and fauna. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e2260008209/galegos-hiking-trail-pr-2-serra-de-sao-mamede" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Galegos Hiking Trail (PR 2) — Serra de São Mamede&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route that explores the natural beauty near the Portuguese park.
tips: Very close to the border with Portugal, this megalithic specimen is one of the best preserved in the area and can be easily reached from Valencia de Alcántara following the Molino de La Negra Route.
Very tasty food, a nice ambience and an original Spanish Camarero.
The beauty of this Gothic temple, located in Valencia de Alcántara, earned it the declaration of a historical-artistic monument, a work that is also part of the historical ensemble of the town.
